name: I slipped at work in Salt Lake City yesterday but didn't tell anyone right away, what do I do now?
description: Report to your employer ASAP (within 180 days in Utah), get medical care, and document details to secure benefits.[1]

mainEntity.name: I slipped at work in Salt Lake City yesterday but didn't tell anyone right away, what do I do now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to your employer ASAP (within 180 days in Utah), get medical care, and document details to secure benefits.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Report the injury to your employer immediately, even if delayed, as Utah law allows up to 180 days but sooner protects your claim.[1] Employers must notify their insurer within 7 days, and you'll receive forms like the First Report of Injury (Form 122).[1][2] Key factors: Document everything, get medical attention explaining how/when/where it happened, and request copies of all forms.[1] See a doctor promptly, avoid signing anything without understanding, and consider a workers' comp specialist if issues arise.[2] Prompt action maximizes benefits under Utah's system.[1]
